title: Controversy of the Week Social media: Will jury awards protect kids from damage?...
source: The Guardian - The Week USA – April 10, 2026
content:
Controversy of the Week Social media: Will jury awards protect kids from damage? Big Tech may have reached “an inflection

People Rodrigo’s life on the market Olivia Rodrigo knows dating sucks, said Amel Mukhtar inVogue. The pop star skyrocketed to global stardom at 17 with her 2021 hit “Drivers License,” and the world quickly became obsessed with uncovering the drama behind the lovelorn ballad. At the time, she was not merely cop- ing with a breakup, but also acting on a Disney show, finishing high school, and making her debut album,Sour. “Looking back, I always think, ‘Wow, life will never be as hard as it was when I was 17,’” says Rodrigo, 23. But as her fame grew, dating only got harder. She tried the celebrity dat- ing app Raya but bailed after a month. “I didn’t even see anyone cool! I thought I would see, like, ooh, some really hot actor guy,” she says. “It’s [always] some weird ‘creative director.’ Like, you just don’t have a job.” After one bad date, she tried direct-messaging people she was interested in. “I had a pretty high success rate at the time,” she says. “But what does success mean? Going on a date? Sure. Were they good? No!” Now in a relationship with British actor Louis Partridge, Rodrigo cringes thinking about her earlier misadventures. “I like to pretend they never happened. Relation- ships these days are soweird!” she says. “I’m definitely a lover girl.
